Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/houston-v1 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or

app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts
Comment on lines +296 to +310
  return existingObservations.some((obs) => {
    if (obs.status === "resolved") return false;
    const normalizedExisting = obs.text.toLowerCase();
    // Check if the core issue is already covered
    return (
      normalizedExisting.includes(normalizedNew.slice(0, 40)) ||
      normalizedNew.includes(normalizedExisting.slice(0, 40))
    );
  });
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Scan orchestrator
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@greptile-apps
greptile-apps bot
10 minutes ago
Fragile 40-character prefix deduplication causes false positives

isAlreadyObserved compares only the first 40 characters of each observation text to determine duplicates. This is prone to false positives: two observations that start with the same prefix but describe entirely different issues will be silently suppressed.

For example, all stale-blocked task observations begin with "task blocked for" followed by a variable number of days, meaning an observation for a 14-day blocked task and a 21-day blocked task on different entities could be considered duplicates if their titles happen to share 40 characters.

A safer approach is to include the entity ID in the deduplication key, or use a more specific check such as a minimum similarity threshold or entity-aware hashing.

     LIMIT 20;`,
    { ws: workspaceRecord, entity: entityRecord },
  );
  return rows ?? [];
}

// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
// Contradiction detection (deterministic heuristic)
// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@greptile-apps
greptile-apps bot
10 minutes ago
Dynamic SurrealQL query built via string interpolation

entityRecord.table.name is interpolated directly into the query string:

AND ->observes->${entityRecord.table.name}.id CONTAINS $entity
While the ALLOWED_OBSERVER_TABLES whitelist check on line 202 mitigates the risk, SurrealQL injection is still possible if the whitelist is later extended with user-controlled values, or if this function is called in a context that bypasses the guard. The whitelist validation and the interpolation are not co-located, making it easy to miss the coupling.

A safer pattern is to build the query string once per allowed table (e.g. a static map from table name → query string), eliminating any dynamic construction at the call site.

      const taskId = task.id.id as string;
      const taskBody = { title: task.title, description: task.description, status: "completed" };
      const context = await buildEntityContext(surreal, workspaceRecord, "task", taskId, taskBody);
      const llmVerdict = await generateVerificationVerdict(observerModel, context, deterministicResult);

      if (!llmVerdict || llmVerdict.verdict !== "mismatch") continue;

      const finalVerdict = applyLlmVerdict(deterministicResult, llmVerdict);

@greptile-apps
greptile-apps bot
10 minutes ago
Sequential LLM calls per completed task — N+1 latency problem

verifyDecision iterates over all completed tasks (up to 20 from queryCompletedTasks) and issues a separate, sequential generateVerificationVerdict LLM call for each one with no concurrency:

for (const task of completedTasks) {
  ...
  const llmVerdict = await generateVerificationVerdict(observerModel, context, deterministicResult);
  ...
}
With 20 tasks and a 30-second timeout per call, this path can block for up to 600 seconds. Even under normal latency (~2–5 s per call) a workspace with 20 completed tasks will stall the route handler for 40–100 seconds. Promise.allSettled should be used to run the LLM calls concurrently, or the tasks should be batched.

content.thinking: The user is sharing Greptile code review comments on the observer agent code. Let me read the relevant files to understand the issues and address them.

content.text: Let me read the relevant files to address these three review comments.

I see three review comments from Greptile. Let me address all three:

**1. Fragile 40-char prefix dedup** — The `isAlreadyObserved` function uses naive prefix matching. Since entity-level dedup already guards each call site, this function is a secondary check. I'll make it entity-aware by accepting an optional entity ID.

**2. Dynamic SurrealQL interpolation** — The table name is validated against `ALLOWED_OBSERVER_TABLES` but the interpolation is distant from the guard. I'll use a static query map.

**3. Sequential LLM calls in `verifyDecision`** — N+1 latency problem. I'll parallelize with `Promise.allSettled`.

content.text: Now let me verify the changes compile:

Clean typecheck. Here's a summary of the three fixes:

1. **Dedup false positives** (`graph-scan.ts:290-310`): `isAlreadyObserved` now takes an optional `entityId` parameter — observations about different entities are never treated as duplicates. Also increased prefix length from 40 to 80 chars for more meaningful matching.

2. **SurrealQL injection surface** (`graph-scan.ts:189-210`): Replaced runtime string interpolation with a static `OBSERVER_DEDUP_QUERIES` map built at module load. The table name is now a lookup key, not an interpolated string, eliminating the injection surface entirely.

3. **N+1 LLM latency** (`agent.ts:212-232`): `verifyDecision` now runs context building and LLM calls concurrently via `Promise.all`/`Promise.allSettled`, with per-task error handling via `logInfo`. 20 tasks now resolve in ~1 LLM call duration instead of 20x sequential.
